title: Federal Gray
subtitle: The market stopped taking your calls. The government did not.
summary: You extracted brilliance out of the airline right up to the moment the paperwork acquired handcuffs.

title: Escorted from the Boardroom
subtitle: You kept the company alive just long enough for someone else to blame you.
summary: The creditors, directors, and press found one point of agreement: you were now a legacy cost.

title: Papered Over
subtitle: A larger carrier bought the slots, the gates, and your deniability.
summary: The airline disappeared into a cleaner logo while you left with enough money to call it strategic.

title: Respectable Exit
subtitle: You sold the optimism before reality repriced the story.
summary: The company remained someone else's problem. Your wealth remained very much your own.

title: Nassau Weather
subtitle: The heat got real, so you chose latitude over testimony.
summary: Employees, lenders, and reporters all wanted answers. They received tropical silence.
